entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Excel Add-in ; Customization of the ribbon
entry.content:
Administrative Functions PLAN_VIEW is by default open the authorization for both the Planning View and Data Input section options in the Excel Add-in. This authorization only will help the user to create, edit and interact with the planning view. As PLAN_VIEW function to consolidate Create, Edit and Manage Planning View, we can't have a scenario where user is authorized to create planning view, but not use edit feature or data input section completely.

Best Regards,

Riyaz

entry.title: Re: Sorting in Editable smart table
entry.content:
Hello All ,

for someone who is facing similar issue . i managed to achieve the behavior mentioned below 

In View  : i added a separate button with dialog for sorting 

<!--Button to sort Proposal Quanity 
                                    <Button
                                        id="openBtn"
                                        icon="sap-icon://sort"
                                        press="onCustomSort"
                                    />
                                    <p13n:Popup
                                        id="p13nPopup"
                                        title="Sort fields on local data"
                                        close="onp13nPopupClose"
                                    >
                                        <p13n:panels>
                                            <p13n:SortPanel
                                                id="sortPanel"
                                                title="Sort"
                                            />
                                            
                                        </p13n:panels>
                                    </p13n:Popup>

which renders a dialog similar to below 

in Controller 

  onp13nPopupClose(oEvt) {
            if (oEvt.getParameter("reason") == "Ok") {
                this.applyCustomSort();
            },
      applyCustomSort(oEvt) {
            const oView = this.getView();
            const oP13nState = {
                sort: oView.byId("sortPanel").getP13nData()
            };
            var oCustomTable = this.getView().byId("customTable");
            // Get the binding of the table
            var oBinding = oCustomTable.getBinding("items");
            var aData = oBinding.getAllCurrentContexts().map(function (oContext) {
                return oContext.getObject();
            });
            // Define a comparison function based on column values
            var compareFn = function (a, b, fieldName, descending) {
                var valueA = a[fieldName];
                var valueB = b[fieldName];

                // Handle comparison for different data types
                if (isNaN(valueA) === true && isNaN(valueB) === true) {
                    return descending ? valueB.localeCompare(valueA) : valueA.localeCompare(valueB);
                } else if (isNaN(valueA) === false && isNaN(valueB) === false) {
                    return descending ? valueB - valueA : valueA - valueB;
                }
                else {
                    return 0;
                }
            };
            // Prepare the sort objects array
            oP13nState.sort.forEach(function (item) {
                if (item.name == "PROPOSAL_QUANTITY") {
                    // Sort the items array
                    aData.sort(function (a, b) {
                        return compareFn(a, b, item.name, item.descending);
                    });
                }
            });

            //   Update the table rows based on the sorted items
            oCustomTable.removeAllItems();
            // Get the ColumnListItem template
            var oTemplate = oCustomTable.getBindingInfo("items").template;
            //    Add sorted items back to the table
            aData.forEach(function (data) {
                // Clone the template for each item
                var oItem = oTemplate.clone();
                oItem.setBindingContext(new sap.ui.model.Context(oCustomTable.getModel(), oCustomTable.getModel().createKey("/SmartStockReplenishment", data)));
                oCustomTable.addItem(oItem);
            });
        },

entry.title: A Human-Centred Approach to Identify and Define Business AI Use Cases
entry.content: In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses are keen on integrating Artificial Intelligence into their workflows; yet starting with a clear purpose is crucial. The SAP AppHaus introduces two innovative workshop formats, the Business AI Explore and Business AI Discover/Design, crafted to guide businesses in identifying and defining impactful AI use cases tailored to their specific needs. These workshops emphasize understanding existing business challenges first, to better explore how AI can enhance workflows and operations. This webinar offers a comprehensive overview of both formats, providing practical examples, actionable tips, and access to materials for conducting your own sessions. Join us to discover how to effectively harness generative AI in alignment with business needs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: History and Metrics of All Channel Availability neede...
entry.content:
Hi,

To ensure the PI Monitoring application runs smoothly without any performance issues, data retention periods for availability metrics are fixed ones.

These are the standard PI Monitoring retention periods:

  • Hourly data   -> Retention Period = 24 Hours
  • Daily data     -> Retention Period = 7 Days
  • Weekly data  -> Retention Period = 2 Weeks
  • Monthly data -> Retention Period = 2 Months
  • Yearly data    -> Retention Period = 2 Years

This information is referenced in KBA 3029156 - Housekeeping details of PI Message overview data.

Thank you,

Conor

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to validate the presence of a mandatory subnode in a RAP BO?
entry.content: Hi @patrick_winkler, I'm not sure what needs to be mapped? Since the validation is defined on the child node, the keys parameter contains the primary keys of the child node only, not the parent key(s). Mapping the primary key of the child node to the primary key of the parent node would not result in a valid result. The opposite could work, to read starting from the child using its "to_parent" association, however in this case it does not return any data since we're dealing with the delete trigger.

entry.title: Order related IB vs Delivery related IB in Sales. Double IB documents
entry.content:
Hello All. I have a query related to Intercompany Billing in Sales. We use billing relevance " I " which is order related fir billing plan. During issuing invoice to customer we have subsequently generated IB billing document for order (through RDIV) . Then when we generate deliveries (for tracking purposes since it's Non Stock material) we get  Delivery relevant for Intercompany Billing (with IBS status A  (LIKP -FKIVK). So from logical point of view system tries to make double IB processing since one IB document has already been generated on SO level. 

We have explored solution to change delivery status (from A to Blank) through user exit. But is there any way/ explanation from customizing point of view? Why system allows to make this "double" IB request... Is there anyway to make it not-relevant for deliveries? 

Appreciate your thoughts

entry.title: Re: Sorting in Editable smart table
entry.content:
That is an interesting approach, thanks for sharing your code.

What you could also consider is using a JSON model. JSON models are good for anything you want to do on the client side only. Read data from backend into a JSON model, bind the table to your JSON model and let the table do the sorting, filtering, etc.

That saves you from having to code sorting logic. But at Save, you will have to read from JSON and call the oData save.

Just an option. I've used JSON models a lot (eg. Planning Calendar date calculations) and find then flexible for client-side manipulation.

Ramin.

entry.title: Re: How to import data via Integration Center that has co...
entry.content:
I spoke with SAP support (via Expert Chat) about this and there was not a straightforward solution to allow an Integration Center job to accept values that have commas.

As a workaround, we put different values in the columns that contained columns and then added a Value Lookup Table to the IC job to translate to the appropriate value - with commas...  This worked as expected once it was all set up. Just took some additional time to set up the lookup tables and adjust the import file.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2405 Now Available – What’s New?
entry.content:
The latest update of SAP Service and Asset Manager introduces many exciting features and configuration options across all supported personas.

New streamlined personas, enhanced meter management, and SAP Dynamic Forms integration elevate mobile maintenance and field service capabilities, alongside powerful new online search capabilities. Improved user interfaces, updates for safety technicians, and advanced inventory management features will ensure precision and accountability across your mobile workflows.Also, we continue to roll out noteworthy customer-requested features for all maintenance execution personas, and expand configuration options on the backend.

Please read on to learn more.

Key features delivered in the 2405 release

Standard and Professional Personas

The SAP Service and Asset Manager maintenance technician enables detailed end-to-end maintenance execution procedures on a mobile device with access on the backend to configurable features to supplement additional business requirements like vehicle stock, mileage, expenses, etc. However, some customers want a simpler mobile application where technicians and floor supervisors may report and manage maintenance requests with access to asset data and related details

This release introduces new configurations for our maintenance and field service personas, separating them into standard and professional categories. The streamlined standard personas are licensed at ½ FUE, and professional personas at 1 FUE.

Let’s look at each persona separately.

Maintenance technician

  • Professional: Same long-standing persona from previous releases; absolutely no change in previously available features or functionality.
  • Standard (new): Focused on notification processing with features like core data, measurements, attachments, and geospatial; does not include advanced features such as supervisor role, signature capture, and expenses, nor integrations like linear asset management and routes.

Field service technician

  • Professional (new): Includes all features and functionality in the standard persona; adds meter management capabilities, technical object bill of materials and classifications, and the ability to create/edit technical objects.
  • Standard: Same persona features and functionality available since the 2205 release.

Learn more about persona configuration on the SAP Help Portal

Meter management*

Meter management has been available since the 2.0 release of SAP Asset Manager in 2018 as an add-on component. The component has since been rolled into the core application, enabled via feature flags in the Mobile Application Integration Framework (MAIF).

With this release, meter management has been extended to the field service professional persona since orders associated with meter management can fall into both maintenance and service categories, depending on the specific scenario and how the SAP system is configured.When enabled, field service and maintenance technicians may download, display, and execute meter orders; install, uninstall, replace, connect, and disconnect meters; and take periodic and aperiodic readings.

We improved the user experience of the install/uninstall process. Technicians may now install/uninstall multiple meters from a single work order, and several steps have been combined resulting in few clicks such as updating a meter and recording a reading in a single screen.

Online search for equipment and functional locations†

In our ongoing effort to add more online features, this release introduces an online search for equipment and functional locations. Online search is available on the home page, in the side menu, and from the equipment and functional location list pages.

Having this search provides advantages for businesses and users. The number of technical objects that are downloaded in a sync can be limited only to those most relevant to the technician’s work, this improving sync times. Additional objects then may be downloaded as needed.

Objects can be search by several criteria. A single object can be searched by inputting or scanning the object ID, or retrieve a larger set searching by description, category, plant details, location, work center, and/or status. A list of matching objects is presented to the technician who then may download objects to the device.

SAP Dynamic Forms‡ support

A highly anticipated upcoming addition to our SAP Mobile Service and Asset Management portfolio is SAP Dynamic Forms, a platform for creating and managing digital forms built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Templates can be designed with form fields and validation rules and then assigned to work orders and operations. As part of the service, the forms may be accessed in a web browser or directly from SAP Service and Asset Manager.

Form instances associated with work orders, operations, and suboperations will be download to the device and may be completed offline during work execution. Technicians may save drafts as they complete their work, submitting the final form and syncing after completion.

Completed forms are synced back to SAP BTP where post-processing can occur, such as mapping captured data to business objects and triggering follow up actions.

Additional features

User experience improvements 

The user experience of our application has taken a front seat in our recent releases, and we continue to gather feedback from customers and partners through ongoing research. This release continues this trend with several significant updates.

Detail page enhancements

Throughout the application, work order, operation, and operational item detail pages have been updated to improve efficiency. You will now find scrollable card sections representing operations and parts where common actions may be taken without navigating.

Common objects are represented by new colorful icons to easily differentiate the type, e.g., work order, equipment, functional location, part, etc.†

Documents are now shown individually in a dedicated section on the detail page accompanied by a document preview.† The attachment may be opened and viewed, and new attachments added without navigating to a separate page.

Measurement readings with editable data table

In our last release we introduced the editable data table for completing checklists and inspections. The table format has now been expanded to measurement readings.

Technicians can seamlessly input measurements directly into the table, significantly expediting workflows. A sticky column option facilitates smooth horizontal and vertical scrolling while ensuring crucial data remains visible regardless of navigation.
Users may set their preferences opting for the new table or the traditional list views.

Safety technician features

Introduced a year ago, the safety technician persona ensures the safety of workers by isolating switches, valves, or circuit breakers connected to equipment. We continue to introduce new features desired by our customers.

Ad hoc operational items†

During the isolation process, technicians may identify additional points that may not be reflected on the safety certificate. Being able to add new operational items on the mobile device saves time.

With this release, a technician may view a safety certificate, revoke the prepared status, and add additional operational items to ensure all isolation points are reflected. Details of the new operational item can be recorded such as switching and physical location; operational group; as well as tagging condition, sequence, and comment.

The certificate can be updated to prepared after additional items have been added.

Work permit approval signatures

Signatures can now be attached when approving work permits providing an extra level of accountability to the approval process. The signature is saved as an attachment to the permit and is watermarked with the certificate ID and description, approver ID, date, and time.

This is an optional feature that can be configured on the backend.

Inventory clerk features

This release includes new inventory features devised and designed in collaboration with our stakeholders. Our dual goals are 1) to improve the user experience based on real world scenarios, and 2) to expand support for movement types available in SAP Materials Management (MM).

Goods issue and receipt bulk updates

On the user experience front, we introduce a new tabular mass entry screen for issuing and receiving goods.

When processing inbound and outbound goods, the inventory clerk may choose to issue all or receive all respectively. The order details can be edited before proceeding to the mass entry table where items can be selected/deselected individually. Storage location, quantity, and UOM for each item may be updated in a single screen.

A link is also provided for each item so additional details, e.g., stock type, special stock indicator, storage bin, etc., can be updated optionally.

Attachments and signature

The application now supports attachments and signature for material documents, enhancing the documentation, tracking, and verification processes.

The clerk may take one or more photos of an outgoing or incoming shipment. The images may be cropped, annotated, and resized before syncing. The signature is saved as an attachment on the material document.

Both are optional features that can be configured on the backend.

Additional movement types

Two-step transfers† are useful for tracking the movement of goods when the transfer takes time or when goods need to be accounted for throughout the transfer process. It involves two distinct phases: 1) the removal of stock from the sending location, and 2) the receipt of stock at the receiving location.

We now support movement types 303 (first step) and 305 (second step) used when transferring stock from one plant to another within the same company code.

Additional reversals codes are also supported.

Movement types 262 (reversal of goods issue for cost center) and 282 (reversal of goods issue to project) may be used to reverse a goods issue when there was an error in the initial posting or when the goods were not actually used or consumed. This ensures accuracy in inventory and financial records by correcting or canceling the initial goods issue.

Personalization

Filter persistence†

By default, SAP Service and Asset Manager will now remember the last filtered state of list pages. The user may navigate away from the list page and then return to the same filtered set.

This can be disabled in the profile settings.

Quick action button personalization

Context-aware quick action buttons were introduced in our last release to minimize unnecessary navigation and reduce the number of clicks required to perform essential tasks.

These buttons may now be customized based on user preference. A settings icon appears next to each button set where buttons may be disabled if desired.

ESRI ArcGIS named users

The application now supports authentication for individual users using ArcGIS named user authentication. The user can log in using their unique credentials with the access token securely saved on the client.

Once logged in, users can view authenticated business and reference layers based on their ArcGIS type, role, and privileges.

Release available now

The application is publicly available and includes an offline demo mode for viewing and testing the extensive functionality with maintenance technician, field service technician, safety technician, and inventory clerk personas included.

The application metadata is now available for licensed customers and partners on the SAP Support Portal Software Center and the mobile client may be downloaded from the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.

Share your ideas

We encourage you to learn more about submitting improvement requests to influence future releases of SAP Service and Asset Manager. Your ideas are the driving force behind our innovation.

Explore other SAP mobile applications

SAP has a powerful suite of SAP native mobile applications. Follow the MXE Community for latest updates.

* In scope only for professional and custom personas

† SAP Customer Influence request

‡ Coming soon. SAP Dynamic Forms is included with SAP Service and Asset Manager license with fixed entitlement of 1200 documents per FUE/year; tiered add-on licensing available for blocks or 1000 documents.

entry.title: Collecting customer feedback at scale for SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Continuously delivering fit-for-purpose solutions for customers' jobs-to-be-done requires broad and deep knowledge of customers needs and experience with the product. For this, we rely on engaging with customers and capturing feedback.

At SAP Commerce Cloud, we collect feedback via a number of channels throughout the product development lifecycle. While engaging with customers one-on-one gives us great insights, it is essential that we also scale these engagements and hear what works well and what needs improvement from a wider group of customers. To scale feedback collection, we look to the Customer Loyalty (NPS) Survey, as well as the SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence Sessions, and Embedded Product survey, to hear from the masses across different personas from decision-makers to implementers and day-to-day business users. We continuously review this feedback and consider it as part of our roadmap planning process. 

SAP Customer Loyalty (NPS) Survey

The Customer Loyalty (NPS) Survey is sent by SAP to eligible customers once a year to measure loyalty and product satisfaction. It provides a Net Promoter Score (NPS), which is a standard customer experience metric that is calculated based on customers’ response to the question: “How likely are you to recommend SAP to colleagues or business acquaintances?”. Based on the rating given between 0 (not at all likely) and 10 (extremely likely), it puts respondents into 3 different categories:

  • Promoters (score 9-10): are loyal enthusiasts who will keep buying and fuel growth by referring others
  • Passives (score 7-8): are satisfied but unenthusiastic customers who are vulnerable to competitive offerings
  • Detractors (score 0-6): are unhappy customers who can damage the brand and impede growth through negative word-of-mouth

The NPS is calculated simply by subtracting the percentage of detractors from the percentage of promoters, resulting in a score between -100 to +100, where the highest score is more desirable. The NPS and product satisfaction scores and comments give us an opportunity to listen to customers and understand pain points and what works well, in order to make investment decisions and improvements in the product and at every level of the organisation to address that feedback.

SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence Sessions

In Q4, 2022, we opened the SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence Sessions, which are available on the SAP Customer Influence Portal. Through this channel, we invite SAP Commerce Cloud customers and partners to post and vote for improvements they would like to see delivered in the product. As opposed to the Customer Loyalty (NPS) Survey, where we are only on the receiving end of feedback, the SAP Customer Influence Portal allows product management to engage directly with the requester and voters and ask clarifying questions to better understand how to deliver on needs. This helps us turn sometimes vague feedback into actionable requirements, which we can more accurately respond to.

Over the last year, we have received more than 150 Improvement Requests and close to 1000 votes from 125 unique customers and partners. 22% of these Improvement Requests have progressed, meaning they have either been delivered, or we are planning on delivering them, or they have been closed because they are either already offered or will not be offered as part of the product.

This is a great channel for SAP Commerce Cloud customers to engage with our product management team and provide us with actionable feedback, as well as indicating interest in improvements through votes. See this Knowledge Base Article to learn how to access this channel.

 

Turning your feedback into action

Based on the feedback collected through the above-mentioned channels, we would like to share some recent and planned deliverables which are directly in response to what we are hearing from you. We have split the response into six categories, focusing on the product itself and user assistance. Please refer to each section below to find a summary and a link to a recording with further details. 

SAP Commerce Cloud: Release Strategy

For a better overview of new SAP Commerce Cloud releases and the impact of changes introduced, and to address project complexities and version compatibility across components, the product and user assistance teams have provided a number of improvements to the SAP Help Portal documentation. 

The team is also monitoring incidents and engaging directly with customers, as well as partners and SAP Services, to learn about challenges first-hand. In addition, deliverables, such as Blue/Green and Canary deployments, as well as performance testing, are making it easier for customers to adopt and stay up-to-date on the latest releases.

Hear SAP Commerce Cloud Product Manager, Oana Ginghina, explain how you can use the documentation to guide you in taking 2211 Update Releases.

SAP Commerce Cloud: Cloud Automation

Cloud Automation is shipping several deliverables in 2024 to make it easier for SAP Commerce Cloud customers to manage their environments and deployments. Available to customers as of April, 2024, is the ability to purchase consumption credits to self-service scale non-production environments for testing purposes in the Cloud Portal. Later this year, customers will be able to perform safer deployments to production with Canary deployments, building on the already delivered Blue / Green deployment mechanism. Canary deployments allow for new deployments to be tested on a small population of customers before being rolled out to all. Zonal redundancy will also be made available to customers to help save costs caused by availability zone interruptions.

Hear SAP Commerce Cloud Product Manager, Conner Helton, speak about these highly requested additions for successfully operating SAP Commerce Cloud.

SAP Commerce Cloud: Observability

In the area of observability, there are also several enhancements being delivered to improve on the operation of SAP Commerce Cloud. To better manage surge events, such as large marketing campaigns or holiday sales, customers now have access to dashboards and a Surge Event Preparation Checklist for guidance on how to prepare for and monitor these events. Customers have also asked for more flexibility with Dynatrace, as well as the possibility to integrate Dynatrace data with 3rd party systems. For enhanced flexibility, customers will be able to monitor key request and external services separately, and for enhanced integrations, customers will be able to poll Dynatrace data in external systems, plus receive notifications through alerting systems such as SNOW, in addition to app push notifications and emails. 

Hear SAP CX Monitoring Product Manager, Samir Guermach, share more details on the enhancements provided for monitoring of SAP Commerce Cloud. 

SAP Commerce Cloud: Integration

SAP Commerce Cloud offers a large set of pre-built integrations, based on use case-driven integration offerings and requests, as well as reference architectures for end-to-end scenarios. Customers can use these to guide their implementations. To simplify the integration of SAP solutions further, SAP is investing heavily in providing integrations based on aligned APIs and shared meta models.

Hear SAP CX Integrations Product Manager, Karsten Hartmann, give an overview of available SAP Commerce Cloud integrations and reference architectures, and speak about enhancements to integrations based on aligned APIs. 

SAP Commerce Cloud: Business Functionality

When it comes to business functionality, our customers generally agree that SAP Commerce Cloud offers a mature set of features across the board. However, we do hear that payment integration requires significant effort to implement, and as a response to that, there are plans to introduce the Open Payment Framework as a low code / no code solutions to easily integrate SAP Commerce Cloud with any 3rd party payment service provider with much less effort.

Other key functional innovations planned for 2024/25 and related to the modernisation of the platform, are the new search service, including intelligent personalisation capabilities, and an integration with the Omnichannel Promotion Pricing Services. 

Hear SAP Commerce Cloud Product Manager, Rick Hobbs, speak about these new services and how they support customers in modernising and simplifying how they run commerce. 

SAP Commerce Cloud: User Assistance

As outlined in the SAP Commerce Cloud Release Strategy section, a new consolidated Upgrade Releases guide is now available on SAP Help Portal to support the successful adoption of SAP Commerce Cloud 2211 Continuous Innovation releases. Customers can also subscribe to the What’s New Viewer to stay up to date on new content. Other requests are to add more videos to documentation and more user-friendly API documentation, so expect to see continuous improvements in these areas in 2024. 

Hear SAP Commerce Cloud User Assistance Lead, Andrew Irvine, speak about these initiatives and guide you as to where you can provide detailed feedback on user assistance improvements. 

Support Improvements

In addition to highlighting the product and user assistance deliverables, we would like to take this opportunity to mention actions the Technical Support team has taken in response to feedback from the Customer Loyalty (NPS) Survey. 

To reduce response time and provide easy access to expertise, the support team provides the Expert Chat for Commerce covering most of the SAP Commerce Cloud components. In additional, Schedule an Expert service offers a new way to connect with an SAP Technical Support expert in a live, one-on-one 30 minute call at a time of your choosing.

To better serve Priority One issues, technical support will call the reporter if needed and required for clarifications, as well as ensure warm handovers during shift changes. Customers can also provide direct feedback on Support Quality through the Schedule a Manager feature. Customers are highly encouraged to complete the Support Survey whenever the ticket is closed.

 

Our ask to you as a customer

Successfully prioritising deliverables is dependent on the quality of feedback and data we are able to collect from our customer base at scale. We highly appreciate your engagements and want to ensure you that we are continuously analysing and evaluating the feedback you provide us with, so please, continue to respond to the the SAP Customer Loyalty (NPS) and satisfaction surveys, engage with us in the Continuous Influence Sessions on SAP Customer Influence Portal by posting and voting for improvements requests, and make sure to stay current on 2211 with the latest Update Releases to take advantage of fixes, security updates, and new innovations.

Please share your feedback on this article in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Further Update: Lean Services Procurement - Commerce Automation

Unplanned Lean Service items are only supported with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
Even for Planned Lean Services, kindly note that 'lean' information can only be mapped with use of cXML and not IDocs. Feature documentation & few SAP Help links do mention On-Premise system support but that may only work if cXML is being used for document transfer. So, in a standard setup, this also is only supported with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Activate SAP SuccessFactors SCIM IPS connector for Permission-Based User Sync for Story Reporting
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We were in the process of Story Reports enabling wherein we identified a minor configuration difference from the SAP docs referred. As per SAP doc migrating to permission-based user sync and Implementation Design Principles, in order to enable Story reports, we would need to perform IPS user sync and for the sync activity, it is important to know if our systems are configured with ODATA IPS connector or SCIM IPS connector to synchronize users between SAP SuccessFactors, IAS, and SAP Analytics Cloud tenant. We can  can validate the IPS connector currently in use if is ODATA IPS connector or SCIM IPS connector with the following steps:

  1. Navigate to IPS and click on Source Systems.
  2. Select relevant SuccessFactors source.
  3. Click the Properties pill.
  4. Verify the API version.
  5. If sf.api.version is set to 2, you are using SCIM IPS connector.
  6. If sf.api.version is set to 1, you are using ODATA IPS connector. Please double check the URL
    and you will notice the domain is appended with /odata/

However, on validating the same with our IPS setup, we identified that for our environment Source Systems, the API version field (sf.api.version) is not available in Properties pill. PFA screenshot for reference.

It would be really helpful if anybody could confirm if the API version not being available in IPS source system is going to impact or stand as a blocker for Story Reports enablement and what would be the recommended SAP approach for the Permission-Based User Sync for stories in this particular scenario.

Looking forward to some much helpful responses.
Thanks in advance for your time and support!!

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Teams Integration with SuccessFactors - New 'Home' tab in the SuccessFactors App
entry.content:
Hello Venessa,

I left some of my thoughts here before I saw this blog Some issues with the SuccessFactors Microsoft Team... - SAP Community

In brief, the biggest complaints I have today with the app are the lack of support and the external links in the app to help.SAP. I have experienced errors within the app and there is no clear support path for how to resolve them. (When to go to SAP vs internal IT vs something you can do in SF, and if the issue is with SF, having support resources within SF that can handle app issues). I ended up figuring out independently that the user mapping errors some of our users were experiencing were caused by their email address being used by two different users in the system

We really don't want our end users going to help.SAP and seeing additional actions which cannot be completed in the application because we don't have them enabled, such as the view pay statement or time sheet. This is just going to raise a bunch of questions

This happens when you type "help" into the app. It provides to links to help.sap. We'd love to disable this.

The new app experience was relatively easy to set up, though our internal IT is wary about how many notifications the application is going to send to the MS teams activity feed, and the added documentation since its original release was sufficient to pass our internal security review.

The functionality is currently limited because of our limited use of EC quick actions, though we plan to expand our quick action usage with some targeted actions soon. I also assume the pay statement feature will be useful once we are live with ECP

I should also mention that the app was installed by some of our employees before it was ever connected to our SF instance. I think they left some negative reviews on the app store as a result of it not working. I don't think the messaging is clear when it cannot connect to an SF instance that our company hasn't set it up yet. We eventually disabled the app from the app store before connecting it, but there as a period when we didnt realize it was available but some of our users did

entry.title: Governance of application-agnostic legal business-partner hierarchies
entry.content:
Introduction

When you decide to do business with a specific supplier or not, it is useful to know about the organizational set-up of the company group. And if this knowledge is based on high-quality data, it is even better: this is where governance of legal business partner hierarchies comes in. The same holds true for customer information. If you have corporate group information about a new customer at hand, starting business is a lot easier.   

A unified, trusted view of your business enables you to work more efficiently and make better decisions. SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition helps customers to pull together master data and manage it centrally using a master data management layer based on SAP Business Technology Platform. SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition lets customers:  

  •  Centrally consolidate and manage an application-agnostic legal hierarchy to represent complex organizational and cross-organizational ownership structures of your business partners.
  • Replicate the centrally governed hierarchy information for consumption across lines of business such as SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle Performance.  

Generally, the future target state looks as follows:  

How the current process looks like in detail

Key processes in governing legal business partner hierarchies include: 

Manage core attributes and hierarchy structure, featuring: 

  • Fuzzy search for business partner hierarchies using various criteria. 
  • Create or change process for core master data attributes and structure of business partner hierarchy.  
  • Workflow-based governance process including dual control and email notification to requestor about the progress of the request. 
  • Integrated duplicate check to avoid creation of duplicate hierarchy nodes. 
  • Tracking of changes (who changed what and when). 
  • Automatic replication of activated data to other applications. 

Fig.: Legal BP Hierarchy

Import of core attributes of business partner hierarchy data, featuring:

  • Loading business partner hierarchy data from files.
  • Automated validity checks with optional expert intervention.

Integration of business partner with hierarchy nodes, featuring: 

  • Streamlined integration process enabling the assignment of existing business partners to hierarchy nodes. 
  • Real-time validation checks to ensure the accuracy and integrity of assigned business partners. 
  • Automated success or error notifications to stakeholders. 

Fig.: Assigning a business partner to hierarchy node

Replication of managed hierarchy data to consuming applications, featuring: 

  • Integration with SAP Master Data Integration services providing a unified sharing layer.
  • Automatic replication of activated data at the end of the process to applications such as SAP Ariba Supplier and Lifecycle Performance (SLP).

The initial focus of goverend legal business partner hierarchies is on procurement, but further LoB applications are planned to be integrated, too. For planned timelines for upcoming innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition and other MDG versions, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the  MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Best,

Markus

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Brinda911 

another aspect of your question: Subscriptions cannot be created "in S/4HANA Cloud" only.
You have to create and maintain the in SAP Subscription Billing. The task of SAP Subscription Billing is to manage the subscription live-cycle and to create the input for invoicing, like e.g. the monthly recurring charges or the usage charges. 

Only if your frontend does the subscription management and (pre-)billing, you could skip the subscriptions and integrate the rated items from your frontend directly into S/4. 

The solution order is the tool to go in case you either have the need to sell different types of products together in one document (like sales, service and subscriptions) or if you want to make use of enhanced orchestration and order management capabilities. 

Otherwise, you might consider creating the subscriptions directly from Salesforce in SAP Subscription Billing using the APIs. 

In both cases you need to make sure, that basic master data like products and customers are synchronised between S/4HANA Cloud and Susbcription Billing according to the provided standard integration.

Best regards
Stefan

I have about 100 computers at various locations all running fine. This one problem child vexes me though. Worst of all, they had the old SP13 runtime version and when I upgraded them, this started. My problem user is on MS Server 12 and SQL 16. Thank you.

entry.title: Overview of SAP Customer Experience solutions Integration and Extension Options
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Customer Experience solutions portfolio includes a variety of cloud-based products that will enable key parts of your business.  To automate your complete end to end business scenarios, you will need to integrate products in the portfolio along with other SAP products, such as SAP S/4HANA, along with third-party solutions. This article provides some guidelines to choose the ideal integration strategy for various scenarios.

 

 

 

 

 



Table of Contents

  • Integration & Extension Approaches
    • Asynchronous or Point to Point
    • Middleware
    • Cloud Integration
    • Cloud Extension
  • Data & Non-Functional Requirements
    • Transactional Data
    • Master Data
    • Data Format
    • Non-Functional Requirements
  • Conclusion

Integration & Extension Approaches

Integration and extension approaches fall into these general categories:

Asynchronous or Point to Point

This category includes file-based solutions that must be configured for a specific source and destination system.  Examples are ImpEx and Hot Folders for SAP Commerce, or direct OData calls to Marketing, Sales, Service or Commerce Clouds.

Middleware

This category includes solutions that leverage an intermediary system to provide a message bus or other mechanism to orchestrate multiple source and target systems.  Examples include Extract, Transform & Load (ETL) tools, SAP Process Integration / Process Orchestration  or SAP Commerce Cloud, data hub.

Cloud Integration

This category includes cloud-based integration solutions such as SAP Integration Suite and Third Party solutions available in the SAP Store.

Cloud Extension

This category includes cloud-based extension platforms such as the SAP Build Code powered by the Kyma open source project and other SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) services. SAP Build Code provides a cloud-native platform for building services. This includes serverless functions with a services catalog that handles all the security and connectivity to both custom built and SAP BTP services as well as third-party services. It also provides a standard Application Connector model to connect SAP Customer Experience solutions applications with a standard event model and API management.

Data & Non-Functional Requirements

Transactional Data

Integrations can be required for maintaining consistent data across multiple systems (see "Master Data" below), but they could also be needed for transactional data. Examples of these types of integration include quote, order, invoice, payment, fraud, tax, social, and email. 

  • SAP Store: The SAP Store provides pre-built, certified extensions built by partners and service providers for easily integrating services into the SAP Customer Experience solutions applications.
  • Writing your own custom integration: If the integration you want to use is not provided through an available integration API (see https://api.sap.com/) or in the SAP Store, then you're going to have to build the integration yourself. For common transactional integrations, you can integrate with your service provider quicker by using certain APIs.

Master Data

With any integration, there will be shared data. Master data ensures that there is only a single source of truth for the data. The requirements will define which system will hold this data. Any other system that needs this data will need to either replicate the data or have a way to access it. Examples of master data can include one or more of the following:

  • Customer
  • Product
  • Suppliers
  • Stock Level
  • Price

Changes to this data need to be replicated in both systems. For example, if a customer's name has a source of truth in System A, and it is changed in System A, then there will need to be an integration to update the field in System B or enable System B to read the field in System A. It is very important during Foundation and Exploration phases that the master data be defined, as it is a driver for what integrations will be required.

Data Format

Asynchronous integrations can be difficult because the data being interchanged will typically be in different formats. The data itself may also be structured (row-column data model) or unstructured. Examples include web pages, word documents, social feeds, and more). It may be in a common format (for example, comma-separated values) or it could be stored in a proprietary format known only to you. If you are transferring data in or out of SAP Customer Experience solutions applications, a transformation will need to be performed between the source and target systems you're calling. Alternatively, you could build code to make a direct call to or from the target system.

Additionally, the data may require enrichment before it's made visible to the end user. This enrichment can happen during import/export, or it can be completed at a later time as part of other business processes. The level of enrichment should be defined as part of the requirements for each integration. 

Non-Functional Requirements

The following topics should be considered for any integration or extension approach:

  • Extensibility - How easy it to extend and customize a solution if whatever is provided out of the box is not exactly what is needed?
  • Scalability - If the amount of data coming in or going out grows or you need more processing power, how easy is it to grow the solution?
  • Audit and Control - What level of audit and control are provided for the integration? Can it log individual changes? Can you prevent specific users/groups from executing an import or export?
  • Testability - How easy is it to write new tests? What level of testing is provided? Are there included test frameworks that are automated or is testing manual?  
  • Ease of Deployment - How quickly can you set up an integration and start import/export processes? Is it configuration only? Are there any additional applications/hardware required? 
  • Reliability - Does the integration provide any options for fail-over? 
  • Stability - How does it handle unexpected errors/exceptions?

Conclusion

This article outlines the high-level categories of integrations and major topics to consider.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

My name is Ryan and I am currently an ERP consultant of a local ERP product of a consultant firm. I am pursuing to become of SAP consultant in next few years, I think this opportunity would be an amazing way to start me on a new career as an SAP solution designer.


I believe this digital skills initiative is an excellent opportunity to give back and assist others in taking the first step toward transforming their lives. While I am committed to staying motivated in my own learning, I find it even more crucial to pledge to pass on my knowledge and experience, helping to educate others when the opportunity arises.

Thank you for this wonderful opportunity.

Ryan

entry.title: Create LinkedButton in form suppliers invoice
entry.content:
Good morning dear, my code is the following:

if (pVal.EventType == BoEventTypes.et_FORM_LOAD && pVal.BeforeAction ==false)
{

Form oForm = Application.SBO_Application.Forms.GetForm("141", 0);

Item i = oForm.Items.Add("btnlink" + valor.ToString(), BoFormItemTypes.it_LINKED_BUTTON);
i.LinkTo ="U_FIELD"; 
i.Height = 9; // To fit a linkbutton it should be 9 pixel high

i.Left = oItemName2.Left - 19;
i.Top = oItemName2.Top + 4;

// i.Top = oItemName2.Top - 1;//oForm.Height - (oItemName2.Height + 48);
// i.Left = oItemName2.Left + oItemName2.Width + 1; //+ 5
// i.Width = oItemName2.Width - 80;
// i.Height = oItemName2.Height + 1;
LinkedButton lb = (LinkedButton)i.Specific;
lb.LinkedObjectType = "30";
lb.LinkedObject = BoLinkedObject.lf_JournalPosting;

}

When I open more than 1 supplier invoice forms at the same time, the button only works on the first open form and the error Item Already exists appears on the others, please support.

Kind regards.

entry.title: We are facing an issue with a standard SAP transaction.
entry.content:
Hello team, 

A user mistakenly entered the wrong quantity in the operation 0011, user said  " In order: operation 0011 for 160.4L put into stock in COR6, UD08 passed through Operational Quality, cancellation of the operation in CORS of 0011, re-confirmation of operation 0011 in cor6 at 160.5L and then I realized that the quantity in stock had been accumulated... so I redid a CORS to cancel the operation 0011 put into stock." 

She first wants to cancel the document by doing MBST, it did not work. 

Now she wants to do a reverse mvt 102 but it's not working. We've tried with MB31 but it didn't work. 

Could you please help us to solve this case ? 

Thanks you

entry.content:
Hi @craigyu 

 

You could use the API API_BUSINESS_PARTNER and a Patch request to set the archiving and purpose complete flag

 

Afterwards you can use the archiving object CA_BUPA   to delete the Business Partner

 

 

Best Regards

Enda
